text.title: Model Upgrade Migration Playbook Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Apply to a single model-family migration instance. Never batch multiple unrelated decisions (model change + prompt rewrite + new feature) through one pass.
rule.rationale: Batching turns a 1-decision artefact into a debate club. Each instance gets its own record so the rationale is traceable.

rule.statement: Every input field has declared type and source (vendor announcement URL, deprecation date, current model id, target model id). Free-form prose without source citation rejected.
rule.rationale: Untyped inputs let LLMs hallucinate plausible-but-wrong values. Typed + sourced inputs make the output falsifiable.

rule.statement: Output names a single accountable owner (handle, email, or org role). No 'team' or 'we' as owner.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership. Future readers need a named human to chase when migration stalls.

rule.statement: Migration cannot promote without golden-set eval delta within contract (default: ≥-0.02 per metric) AND shadow-traffic gate pass.
rule.rationale: Without golden-set gate, silent regressions ship to users; without shadow gate, cost/latency surprises hit production.

rule.statement: Output includes named rollback plan with: kill-switch flag, baseline model id, traffic-shift step-down sequence, owner. No rollback plan = blocked.
rule.rationale: Production migration without rollback is a one-way door. Kill-switch is the cheapest insurance.

rule.statement: Each prompt in the affected fleet is evaluated individually — pass / regression / improvement — and regressions are triaged before bulk promotion.
rule.rationale: Different prompts react unevenly to model upgrades. Bulk promotion masks the regressions until production.

rule.statement: Skip when migration is config-only (e.g. region change), no production traffic affected, or single-prompt isolated tool.
rule.rationale: Methodology overhead is justified by fleet + traffic surface; isolated changes don't need it.
